title: MetaVelvet Installation and Getting Started Guide
author: Afiahayati
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d5k84v

steps.title: Download and Decompress Source Code
steps.instruction: Download source code from here: http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/src/. Decompress the tar ball using: ~$ tar zxvf MataVelvet-v1.1.01.tgz

steps.title: Alternative: Clone from GitHub
steps.instruction: Alternatively, up-to-date source code is available from the GitHub MetaVelvet project: https://github.com/hacchy/MetaVelvet. Clone it using: ~$ git clone git://github.com/hacchy/MetaVelvet.git

steps.title: Navigate to MetaVelvet Directory
steps.instruction: Change directory to the MetaVelvet directory: ~$ cd MetaVelvet-v1.1.01

steps.title: Compile MetaVelvet
steps.instruction: Compile MetaVelvet execution files. In the MetaVelvet directory, run: make ['MAXKMERLENGTH = k'] ['CATEGORIES = cat']. 'k' is the maximum k-mer size you like to use. 'cat' is the maximum number of read categories (i.e., maximum number of libraries in different insert lengths).

steps.title: Verify Executable Creation
steps.instruction: Then, an executable file, meta-velvetg, will be created (for versions >= 1.1.01).

steps.title: Install Executables
steps.instruction: Copy the executable files to /usr/bin or a directory you prefer for installation. Example: ~/MetaVelvet-v1.1.01$ cp meta-velvetg /usr/bin/

steps.title: Understand MetaVelvet Dependencies
steps.instruction: MetaVelvet (for versions >= 1.1.01) uses velveth and velvetg outputs for metagenomics assembly. Three files: "Sequences", "Roadmaps", and "Graph2" are needed for running meta-velvetg.

steps.title: Download and Decompress Sample Dataset
steps.instruction: Download a small dataset from here: http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/data/. Decompress the tar ball using: ~$ tar zxvf HMP.small.tar.gz

steps.title: Execute velveth
steps.instruction: Execute velveth to import read sequences and construct the k-mer hash table. Please check that "out-dir/Sequences" and "out-dir/Roadmaps" files are created. Note that k-mer size has an important effect on assembly results. If your short read data is longer than 65bp, we recommend a k-mer longer than 51. Example: ~$ velveth out-dir 51 \ -fasta -shortPaired \ HMP.small/SRR041654_shuffled.fasta \ HMP.small/SRR041655_shuffled.fasta

steps.title: Execute velvetg
steps.instruction: Execute velvetg to construct an initial de Bruijn graph. Please check that "out-dir/Graph2" file is created. Please use the -exp_cov auto option, otherwise meta-velvetg cannot be executed. Example: ~$ velvetg out-dir -exp_cov auto -ins_length 260

steps.title: Execute meta-velvetg
steps.instruction: Execute meta-velvetg to output scaffold sequences. Please check that "out-dir/meta-velvetg.contigs.fa" file is created. This FASTA file contains the main assembly results. Example: ~$ meta-velvetg out-dir [-ins_length 260] | tee logfile
